steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The blank describes Helen Marsh's place in historical memory compared to Zielinska's. The key contrast: Zielinska "will always be remembered" — her legacy is permanent and secure. The blank must describe something weaker or less stable than that.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• "deserving": Speaks to moral worthiness, not the security of historical memory. Doesn't contrast with "will always be remembered." ✗
  • "conspicuous": Means highly visible or prominent — this would put Helen's legacy on par with or above Zielinska's, not below it. Wrong direction. ✗
  • "uncertain": Means not firmly established. Directly contrasts with "will always be remembered" — Helen's historical memory is less secure, which matches the passage's hedging ("perhaps more ______"). ✓
  • "enduring": Means lasting — this would imply Helen's legacy outlasts Zielinska's, the opposite of what the passage implies. ✗
That matches the correct answer — C is correct.
